Surgery Resident Travel Policy

This policy covers travel reimbursement for categorical residents attending or presenting at regional and national meetings, professional development workshops, and career development activities.

💰
Dept. Contribution
$1,500
Clinical years (yrs 2–6)
🔬
T32 Annual Limit
$2,000
Research year residents
🎓
Prof. Development
$2,000
Total allotment, yrs 3–5
📅
Book Travel
45 Days
Prior to departure
🧾
Submit Receipts
25 Days
After return date
🏨
Max Hotel Nights
2 Nights
Clinical year presenters
Who This Policy Covers
🩺 Resident Presenting Research
▼
ℹ️
The PI must review and approve all abstracts before submission. Discuss travel funding availability with your PI first.
  • All abstracts must be approved by the PI prior to conference submission.
  • T32 research year residents: up to $2,000/year from T32 funds; mentor covers any excess.
  • Non-T32 research year residents: grant-funded when available; department contributes up to $1,500/year otherwise; PI covers the rest.
  • Clinical years (2–6): department contributes up to $1,500 for one trip per year if presenting, on panels, or serving on committees. PI funds additional trips.
  • Medical student travel is covered exclusively by the PI.
📚 Resident Professional Development Gen Surgery / Int'l Thoracic / Int'l Vascular
▼
  • $2,000 total allotment to be used during the 3rd, 4th, or 5th year.
  • Timing must be discussed with and approved by the Program Director and Department Leadership.
  • Travel application must be submitted to the Program Coordinator before booking any travel.
  • Residents are encouraged to apply for AAS society travel awards. If awarded, the department will also contribute up to $1,000 (once per resident).
🏥 Fellowship Professional Development Thoracic / Vascular / Transplant
▼
  • $2,000 total to be used once during fellowship for travel or professional development.
  • Timing requires discussion with and approval from the Program Director and Department Leadership.
  • Travel application must be submitted to the Program Coordinator before booking.
  • Clinical instructor travel is covered by the respective Division and handled by division support staff, subject to faculty sponsor approval.
Funding Details

Funding Limits at a Glance

Understand what the department covers vs. what requires PI or personal funding.

Resident Category Funding Source Dept. Limit Overage Responsibility
Research Year — T32 Grant T32 grant funds $2,000/yr Mentor / PI covers excess
Research Year — Non-T32 Grant (if available), then dept. $1,500/yr PI covers excess
Clinical Years 2–6 (presenting / panel) Department $1,500/trip PI or resident for additional trips
Prof. Dev. — Gen / Int'l Thoracic / Int'l Vascular Department $2,000 total Resident (used in yrs 3–5)
Prof. Dev. + AAS Award AAS + Department supplement +$1,000 One-time department supplement
Fellowship (Thoracic / Vascular / Transplant) Department / Division $2,000 total Used once during fellowship
Medical Students PI exclusively — PI covers all costs
Important Funding Notes
⚠️
Wage requirement: If the resident is not paid a wage or stipend from the grant, travel expenses cannot be paid from that grant.
📋
Additional trips (clinical years): Residents may attend more than one meeting per year, but any additional trips beyond the one department-funded trip must be funded by the PI.
🏆
AAS Awards: Apply for AAS society travel awards to supplement professional development funding. The department will contribute an additional $1,000 if you receive an AAS award (once per resident).
Travel Rules

Booking, Lodging & Expense Rules

Follow these guidelines carefully — non-compliant expenses may not be reimbursed.

📋
Resident, Fellow & Clinical Instructor Travel Application
Official form — must be completed and approved prior to booking any travel
⬇ Download Form
Booking Requirements
✈️ Flights & Booking
▼
🚫
Travel will not be allowed if requested fewer than 2 weeks before departure (unless approved by Program Director or PI).
  • All travel must be booked at least 45 days prior to departure.
  • Clinical resident travel is booked by program coordinators.
  • Research resident travel is booked by the administrative assistant for the Division of Surgical Science.
  • Flight change charges will not be reimbursed unless approved by the Program Director.
  • If you extend a trip personally, you must attach a cost comparison of compliant vs. personal-stay flights to your expense report. The department covers only up to the compliant-flight cost.
📅 Book 45+ days in advance
🏨 Lodging
▼
  • Lodging rates cannot exceed the designated meeting hotel rate.
  • Clinical year residents presenting research: lodging limited to 2 consecutive nights maximum (exceptions by Program Director only).
  • Research residents presenting: more than 2 nights requires PI/Mentor approval.
  • If multiple residents attend the same meeting, they should share rooms when possible and appropriate.
  • Additional hotel nights for personal extensions will not be covered.
🍽️ Meals & Per Diem
▼
ℹ️
Per Diem is processed through the Workday expense system. Claim the daily rate for days you are traveling.
  • Travel meals reimbursed using the Per Diem meal allowance in Workday.
  • Meals provided by the conference must be excluded from reimbursement.
  • Per Diem covers all meals, coffee, and snacks for that day.
  • Group meals will not be reimbursed. Each traveler pays their own expenses.
🚗 Transportation
▼
  • Uber/Lyft/taxi is reimbursable for:
    • Home → airport (and return)
    • Airport → conference hotel (and return)
  • Airport parking is reimbursable — submit receipts.
  • Transportation costs for personal trip extensions will not be covered.
🎟️ Registration Fees
▼
  • Registration fees are limited to early and/or trainee registration rates.
  • The trainee is personally responsible for any cost difference between early and late registration rates.
⚠️
Register early! Late registration surcharges are your responsibility.
Receipts & Reimbursement
🧾 Submitting Receipts
▼
✅
Because flights, hotels, and conference registration are booked by the Department, the main receipts you need to submit are: airport parking, and transportation to/from airport and hotel.
  • Submit receipts to the designated Surgery Department representative within 25 days of your return date.
  • If you extended your trip personally, include a cost comparison of compliant vs. extended-stay travel.
📅 Submit within 25 days of return
Weather & Disruptions
🌩️
If weather threatens major disruptions and airlines/hotels offer exceptions or waivers, make every reasonable effort to return home early — unless you have not yet presented.
Personal Extensions
🚫
If you elect to extend a trip for personal reasons, no additional costs will be covered — including airfare difference, change fees, extra hotel nights, or other related expenses. You must attach a cost comparison to your expense report.
Step-by-Step

Travel Approval Process

Follow these steps in order before booking any travel. Missing a step may delay or deny your reimbursement.

Approval Workflow
1
Discuss with PI (if presenting research) Confirm abstract plans and available funding with your PI before any submission.
2
Get PI approval on abstract All abstracts must be reviewed and approved by the PI prior to submission to the conference.
3
Submit travel application to Program Coordinator Complete the travel application before booking anything.
4
Program Director + Scheduling Chief approval Clinical residents need PD and Scheduling Chief sign-off to ensure all clinical responsibilities are covered. Inform the Chief Resident of your service.
5
Travel is booked by the department Do not book your own flights or hotel — the program coordinator (clinical) or Division of Surgical Science admin (research) books on your behalf.
6
Travel & keep receipts Save receipts for airport parking and all transportation to/from airport and hotel. Flights, hotel, and registration are pre-booked for you.
7
Submit receipts within 25 days of return Forward receipts and Workday Per Diem claim to the designated Surgery Department representative.
Key Deadlines
⏱️ Critical Timelines
▼
  • 45 days before travel: Travel must be booked by this date.
  • 14 days before travel: Minimum lead time to even request travel (PD/PI exception required for anything later).
  • 25 days after return: Receipt submission deadline.
  • Early registration deadline: Register before this — late fees are your responsibility.
Program Coordinators

Reach out to the coordinator for your program with questions about travel applications, booking, or reimbursements.

Who Approves Your Travel
✅ Required Approvals
▼
  • PI: Abstract approval (presenting research), funding authorization, excess cost coverage.
  • Program Director: All clinical resident travel; exceptions to lodging nights, last-minute requests, and flight change waivers.
  • Scheduling Chief: Confirms clinical coverage before travel is approved.
  • Chief Resident of your service: Must be informed once travel is approved (clinical residents).
  • Department Leadership: Required for professional development timing.
Traveler Information

Resident Travel Information Form

The Resident Travel Information form will provide your coordinator with important traveler information that will enable easier booking of flights and hotels for the resident.
